# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Build and Notify

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: read

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      
      - name: Sanitize PR title
        id: sanitize_title
        run: |
          PR_TITLE="${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}"
          SANITIZED_TITLE=$(echo "$PR_TITLE" | tr -cd '[:alnum:][:space:]._-' | head -c 200)
          echo "title=${SANITIZED_TITLE}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "title_base64=$(echo -n "$SANITIZED_TITLE" | base64 -w 0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Sanitize PR description
        id: sanitize_description
        run: |
          PR_BODY="${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}"
          SANITIZED_BODY=$(echo "$PR_BODY" | tr -cd '[:alnum:][:space:][:punct:]\n' | head -c 1000)
          echo "description_base64=$(echo -n "$SANITIZED_BODY" | base64 -w 0)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Extract PR metadata
        id: pr_metadata
        run: |
          echo "pr_number=${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "pr_author=${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "pr_url=${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "branch=${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "base_branch=${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      
      - name: Build application
        id: build
        run: |
          echo "Building PR #${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_number }}: ${{ steps.sanitize_title.outputs.title }}"
          BUILD_STATUS="success"
          BUILD_TIME=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
          echo "status=${BUILD_STATUS}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "timestamp=${BUILD_TIME}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Run tests
        id: test
        run: |
          echo "Running tests for: ${{ steps.sanitize_title.outputs.title }}"
          TEST_STATUS="passed"
          echo "status=${TEST_STATUS}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      
      - name: Generate build artifact metadata
        id: artifact_metadata
        run: |
          TITLE_DECODED=$(echo "${{ steps.sanitize_title.outputs.title_base64 }}" | base64 -d)
          DESC_DECODED=$(echo "${{ steps.sanitize_description.outputs.description_base64 }}" | base64 -d)
          
          cat > build-metadata.json <<'METADATA_EOF'
          {
            "pr_number": "${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_number }}",
            "pr_title": "TITLE_PLACEHOLDER",
            "pr_description": "DESC_PLACEHOLDER",
            "pr_author": "${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_author }}",
            "pr_url": "${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_url }}",
            "branch": "${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.branch }}",
            "base_branch": "${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.base_branch }}",
            "build_status": "${{ steps.build.outputs.status }}",
            "test_status": "${{ steps.test.outputs.status }}",
            "build_timestamp": "${{ steps.build.outputs.timestamp }}",
            "commit_sha": "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}",
            "workflow_run_id": "${{ github.run_id }}"
          }
          METADATA_EOF
          
          python3 -c "
          import json
          import sys
          
          with open('build-metadata.json', 'r') as f:
              data = json.load(f)
          
          title = '''${TITLE_DECODED}'''
          desc = '''${DESC_DECODED}'''
          
          data['pr_title'] = title
          data['pr_description'] = desc
          
          with open('build-metadata.json', 'w') as f:
              json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
          "
          
          cat build-metadata.json
      
      - name: Upload build metadata
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: build-metadata-pr-${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_number }}
          path: build-metadata.json
          retention-days: 30
      
      - name: Create deployment tag
        id: deployment_tag
        run: |
          SAFE_TITLE=$(echo "${{ steps.sanitize_title.outputs.title }}" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | tr ' ' '-' | tr -cd '[:alnum:]-' | cut -c1-50)
          TAG="pr-${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_number }}-${SAFE_TITLE}-${{ github.run_number }}"
          echo "tag=${TAG}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "Deployment tag: ${TAG}"
      
      - name: Send Slack notification
        if: always()
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL" ]; then
            echo "Slack webhook not configured, skipping notification"
            exit 0
          fi
          
          TITLE_DECODED=$(echo "${{ steps.sanitize_title.outputs.title_base64 }}" | base64 -d)
          DESC_DECODED=$(echo "${{ steps.sanitize_description.outputs.description_base64 }}" | base64 -d)
          DESC_TRUNCATED=$(echo "$DESC_DECODED" | head -c 300)
          
          if [ "${{ steps.build.outputs.status }}" = "success" ]; then
            COLOR="good"
            STATUS_EMOJI=":white_check_mark:"
          else
            COLOR="danger"
            STATUS_EMOJI=":x:"
          fi
          
          cat > slack-payload.json <<SLACK_EOF
          {
            "attachments": [
              {
                "color": "${COLOR}",
                "title": "${STATUS_EMOJI} PR #${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_number }}: ${TITLE_DECODED}",
                "title_link": "${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_url }}",
                "fields": [
                  {
                    "title": "Author",
                    "value": "${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_author }}",
                    "short": true
                  },
                  {
                    "title": "Branch",
                    "value": "${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.branch }} → ${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.base_branch }}",
                    "short": true
                  },
                  {
                    "title": "Build Status",
                    "value": "${{ steps.build.outputs.status }}",
                    "short": true
                  },
                  {
                    "title": "Test Status",
                    "value": "${{ steps.test.outputs.status }}",
                    "short": true
                  },
                  {
                    "title": "Description",
                    "value": "${DESC_TRUNCATED}",
                    "short": false
                  },
                  {
                    "title": "Deployment Tag",
                    "value": "${{ steps.deployment_tag.outputs.tag }}",
                    "short": false
                  }
                ],
                "footer": "GitHub Actions",
                "footer_icon": "https://github.githubassets.com/favicon.ico",
                "ts": $(date +%s)
              }
            ]
          }
          SLACK_EOF
          
          curl -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' \
            --data @slack-payload.json \
            "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL"
      
      - name: Send email notification
        if: always()
        env:
          NOTIFICATION_EMAIL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFICATION_EMAIL }}
          SMTP_SERVER: ${{ secrets.SMTP_SERVER }}
          SMTP_USERNAME: ${{ secrets.SMTP_USERNAME }}
          SMTP_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.SMTP_PASSWORD }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$NOTIFICATION_EMAIL" ]; then
            echo "Email notification not configured, skipping"
            exit 0
          fi
          
          TITLE_DECODED=$(echo "${{ steps.sanitize_title.outputs.title_base64 }}" | base64 -d)
          DESC_DECODED=$(echo "${{ steps.sanitize_description.outputs.description_base64 }}" | base64 -d)
          
          cat > email-body.txt <<EMAIL_EOF
          PR Build Notification
          
          PR #${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_number }}: ${TITLE_DECODED}
          
          Author: ${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_author }}
          Branch: ${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.branch }} → ${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.base_branch }}
          
          Build Status: ${{ steps.build.outputs.status }}
          Test Status: ${{ steps.test.outputs.status }}
          Build Time: ${{ steps.build.outputs.timestamp }}
          
          Deployment Tag: ${{ steps.deployment_tag.outputs.tag }}
          
          Description:
          ${DESC_DECODED}
          
          PR URL: ${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_url }}
          Workflow Run: https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
          
          ---
          This is an automated notification from GitHub Actions
          EMAIL_EOF
          
          echo "Email notification prepared (actual sending requires SMTP configuration)"
      
      - name: Comment on PR
        if: always()
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const buildStatus = '${{ steps.build.outputs.status }}';
            const testStatus = '${{ steps.test.outputs.status }}';
            const deploymentTag = '${{ steps.deployment_tag.outputs.tag }}';
            const timestamp = '${{ steps.build.outputs.timestamp }}';
            
            const statusEmoji = buildStatus === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';
            
            const comment = `${statusEmoji} **Build Notification**
            
            **Deployment Tag:** \`${deploymentTag}\`
            **Build Status:** ${buildStatus}
            **Test Status:** ${testStatus}
            **Build Time:** ${timestamp}
            
            **Workflow Run:** [View Details](https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }})
            
            This PR will be tracked in release notes with the deployment tag above.`;
            
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: comment
            });
      
      - name: Generate release notes entry
        run: |
          TITLE_DECODED=$(echo "${{ steps.sanitize_title.outputs.title_base64 }}" | base64 -d)
          
          cat > release-notes-entry.md <<NOTES_EOF
          ### PR #${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_number }}: ${TITLE_DECODED}
          
          - **Author:** @${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_author }}
          - **Deployment Tag:** ${{ steps.deployment_tag.outputs.tag }}
          - **Merged:** Pending
          - **Build:** ${{ steps.build.outputs.status }}
          - **Tests:** ${{ steps.test.outputs.status }}
          
          NOTES_EOF
          
          cat release-notes-entry.md
      
      - name: Upload release notes entry
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: release-notes-pr-${{ steps.pr_metadata.outputs.pr_number }}
          path: release-notes-entry.md
          retention-days: 90
